articles.title: Nippon Life AMC shares surge 6% after Q4 profit jumps 29% to Rs 385 crore
articles.content: Nippon Life India Asset Management shares jumped over 6% following strong Q4 FY26 earnings, with net profit soaring 29% year-on-year. The company also declared a final dividend of Rs 12.50 per share. Robust SIP inflows and AUM growth contributed to the positive outlook, with the stock showing bullish technical indicators.
articles.excerpt: Nippon Life India Asset Management shares jumped over 6% following strong Q4 FY26 earnings, with net profit soaring 29% year-on-year. The company also declared a final dividend of Rs 12.50 per share. Robust SIP inflows and AUM growth contributed to the positive outlook, with the stock showing bullish technical indicators.

articles.title: One MobiKwik Systems shares soar 20% in two sessions. What’s triggering the sharp surge?
articles.content: MobiKwik Systems shares surged on Tuesday. The Reserve Bank of India approved its application for a Non-Banking Financial Company license. This allows the launch of a new lending arm, MobiKwik Financial Services Private Limited. The company plans to expand regulated lending and introduce innovative credit products. This move strengthens its fintech platform strategy.
articles.excerpt: MobiKwik Systems shares surged on Tuesday. The Reserve Bank of India approved its application for a Non-Banking Financial Company license. This allows the launch of a new lending arm, MobiKwik Financial Services Private Limited. The company plans to expand regulated lending and introduce innovative credit products. This move strengthens its fintech platform strategy.

articles.title: Maruti Suzuki Q4 Results FY26 Live Updates: Maruti Suzuki, Eternal among key companies to post Q4 results today. Check net profit, revenue estimates
Cohance Lifesciences shares rocket 43% in just 2 trading sessions. Here’s what is fuelling the surge
articles.content: Cohance Lifesciences shares surged over 43% in two days following the appointment of former Cipla CEO Umang Vohra as Executive Chairman and Group CEO. Vohra, with over three decades of experience, will lead the company's transformation and growth journey. His expertise is expected to bolster Cohance's differentiated presence in the global CDMO market.
articles.excerpt: Cohance Lifesciences shares surged over 43% in two days following the appointment of former Cipla CEO Umang Vohra as Executive Chairman and Group CEO. Vohra, with over three decades of experience, will lead the company's transformation and growth journey. His expertise is expected to bolster Cohance's differentiated presence in the global CDMO market.

articles.title: Rupee slips to 94.47 per dollar with oil-sensitive Asia FX, state-run banks' dollar sales cushion
articles.content: The Indian rupee weakened against the dollar on Tuesday, falling to 94.47 as rising oil prices and stalled energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz due to the U.S.-Iran conflict weighed on sentiment. Traders anticipate continued pressure on the rupee until energy supply disruptions ease, while the Reserve Bank of India is expected to intervene to manage volatility.
articles.excerpt: The Indian rupee weakened against the dollar on Tuesday, falling to 94.47 as rising oil prices and stalled energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz due to the U.S.-Iran conflict weighed on sentiment. Traders anticipate continued pressure on the rupee until energy supply disruptions ease, while the Reserve Bank of India is expected to intervene to manage volatility.

articles.title: City Union Bank shares gain 6% as Q4 profit rises 25% to Rs 360 crore
articles.content: Shares of City Union Bank surged nearly 6% on Tuesday after reporting a 25% YoY rise in Q4FY26 net profit to Rs 360 crore, driven by strong NII growth and improved margins. Robust deposit and advance growth, along with stable asset quality and capital position, supported investor sentiment.
articles.excerpt: Shares of City Union Bank surged nearly 6% on Tuesday after reporting a 25% YoY rise in Q4FY26 net profit to Rs 360 crore, driven by strong NII growth and improved margins. Robust deposit and advance growth, along with stable asset quality and capital position, supported investor sentiment.

articles.title: Are metals turning defensive? 5-year returns rival, even beat traditional safe havens
articles.content: Metal stocks are outperforming defensive sectors, delivering strong returns driven by structural demand shifts and supply constraints.  Factors like EV adoption, infrastructure, and energy transition are fueling this sustained growth, attracting significant FII interest despite broader market caution.  Valuations are mixed, emphasizing the need for selective stock picking.
articles.excerpt: Metal stocks are outperforming defensive sectors, delivering strong returns driven by structural demand shifts and supply constraints.  Factors like EV adoption, infrastructure, and energy transition are fueling this sustained growth, attracting significant FII interest despite broader market caution.  Valuations are mixed, emphasizing the need for selective stock picking.

articles.title: Adani Total Gas shares surge 8% to seven-month high after Q4 results, dividend announcement
articles.content: Adani Total Gas shares saw a significant jump following strong financial results. The company reported a 9% rise in net profit for the January-March quarter of FY26. Revenue also grew by 17% year-on-year. This positive performance, driven by higher volumes, led to an 8% surge in the company's stock price. Adani Total Gas also announced a dividend for its shareholders.
articles.excerpt: Adani Total Gas shares saw a significant jump following strong financial results. The company reported a 9% rise in net profit for the January-March quarter of FY26. Revenue also grew by 17% year-on-year. This positive performance, driven by higher volumes, led to an 8% surge in the company's stock price. Adani Total Gas also announced a dividend for its shareholders.

articles.title: Global Market: Emerging markets face mounting economic pressure amid Iran war disruptions
articles.content: The Iran war's economic ripple effect is hitting emerging markets hard, two months on. Rising inflation, trade disruptions, and fiscal strain are evident globally.  While some commodity exporters see gains, many developing nations face currency depreciation and increased borrowing costs, demanding significant financial aid.
articles.excerpt: The Iran war's economic ripple effect is hitting emerging markets hard, two months on. Rising inflation, trade disruptions, and fiscal strain are evident globally.  While some commodity exporters see gains, many developing nations face currency depreciation and increased borrowing costs, demanding significant financial aid.

articles.title: US Stock Market: Warsh moves closer to Fed leadership as Senate committee vote nears
articles.content: The Senate Banking Committee is scheduled to vote on Kevin Warsh's nomination to lead the Federal Reserve, a key procedural step expected to advance his candidacy to the full Senate. This move comes as the Fed concludes a policy meeting, potentially positioning Warsh to take over by May 15.
articles.excerpt: The Senate Banking Committee is scheduled to vote on Kevin Warsh's nomination to lead the Federal Reserve, a key procedural step expected to advance his candidacy to the full Senate. This move comes as the Fed concludes a policy meeting, potentially positioning Warsh to take over by May 15.

articles.title: PSU bank stocks fall up to 3%. How RBI’s ECL directions can impact lenders
articles.content: PSU bank stocks fell up to 3% on Tuesday after RBI confirmed the rollout of the Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework from April 2027, triggering concerns over higher provisioning and capital impact. Brokerages warn of a potential 5–10% hit to net worth, though phased implementation may soften the immediate blow.
articles.excerpt: PSU bank stocks fell up to 3% on Tuesday after RBI confirmed the rollout of the Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework from April 2027, triggering concerns over higher provisioning and capital impact. Brokerages warn of a potential 5–10% hit to net worth, though phased implementation may soften the immediate blow.

articles.title: Broader markets rally! These smallcap stocks jumped up to 57% in April so far. Do you own any?
articles.content: Broader markets are experiencing a significant rally in April, with smallcap stocks outperforming benchmarks. Several smallcap companies, including Ola Electric, Gallantt Ispat, and Welspun Corp, have seen substantial gains of up to 57% this month, recovering from the March selloff.

Several smallcap companies, including Ola Electric, Gallantt Ispat, and Welspun Corp, have seen substantial gains of up to 57% this month, recovering from the March selloff.

articles.title: Global Market: BOJ keeps interest rates steady amid rising oil-driven inflation risks
articles.content: Japan's central bank maintained its benchmark interest rate at 0.75%, a decision met with internal dissent from three board members who pushed for a hike.  Concerns over escalating inflation, fueled by rising oil prices and global disruptions, are growing.  Despite a projected economic slowdown, policymakers are carefully balancing growth support against persistent price pressures, hinting at potential future tightening.
articles.excerpt: Japan's central bank maintained its benchmark interest rate at 0.75%, a decision met with internal dissent from three board members who pushed for a hike.  Concerns over escalating inflation, fueled by rising oil prices and global disruptions, are growing.  Despite a projected economic slowdown, policymakers are carefully balancing growth support against persistent price pressures, hinting at potential future tightening.

articles.title: US Stock Market: Short sellers double down on US life insurers as private credit risks mount
articles.content: Investors are betting heavily against American life insurance companies. This surge in short selling reflects growing worries about the sector's significant investments in private credit. These less transparent lending markets are raising concerns about potential risks. The insurance industry has doubled its private credit holdings over a decade.
articles.excerpt: Investors are betting heavily against American life insurance companies. This surge in short selling reflects growing worries about the sector's significant investments in private credit. These less transparent lending markets are raising concerns about potential risks. The insurance industry has doubled its private credit holdings over a decade.

articles.title: Bajaj Housing Finance share target price raised after Q4. Here's what Morgan Stanley, other brokerages say
articles.content: Bajaj Housing Finance announced a 14 percent profit increase for the January-March quarter. The company's asset quality remained strong. Loan assets and disbursements saw significant growth. Brokerages highlighted positive management commentary and a strong growth outlook. Competition from banks in the home loan segment is anticipated. The stock has shown recovery after a recent dip.

Competition from banks in the home loan segment is anticipated. The stock has shown recovery after a recent dip.

articles.title: Bullish on power, energy, and BFSI; cautious on IT amid weak earnings: Devarsh Vakil
articles.content: HDFC Securities sees opportunity in India's recovering equity markets. The firm favors power and energy sectors, citing infrastructure growth. Financials, including banks and NBFCs, are also preferred. However, the IT sector faces an underweight stance due to uncertain earnings. Sun Pharma's growth strategy and Varun Beverages' expansion are noted positively.
articles.excerpt: HDFC Securities sees opportunity in India's recovering equity markets. The firm favors power and energy sectors, citing infrastructure growth. Financials, including banks and NBFCs, are also preferred. However, the IT sector faces an underweight stance due to uncertain earnings. Sun Pharma's growth strategy and Varun Beverages' expansion are noted positively.

articles.title: Marico, Radico Khaitan in focus as FMCG navigates inflation-led challenges
articles.content: India's FMCG sector navigates inflation. Input costs are rising, impacting margins. Companies are adjusting prices. Past cycles show resilience and growth for larger players. Innovation and premiumisation are key. Marico and Radico Khaitan show positive business updates. Valuations reflect near-term challenges. The sector's fundamentals remain strong.
articles.excerpt: India's FMCG sector navigates inflation. Input costs are rising, impacting margins. Companies are adjusting prices. Past cycles show resilience and growth for larger players. Innovation and premiumisation are key. Marico and Radico Khaitan show positive business updates. Valuations reflect near-term challenges. The sector's fundamentals remain strong.

articles.title: Technical stock picks! 3 shares that could give up to 16% return this week
articles.content: Investors can look at three stocks for potential gains this week. Tata Power, AU Small Finance Bank, and Steel Authority of India show strong technical indicators. These selections suggest possible returns ranging from 8% to 16%. The analysis highlights breakouts and positive momentum as key drivers for these recommendations. Market watchers are observing these opportunities.

articles.title: Coal India shares rise over 3% after Q4 results: What Jefferies, Morgan Stanley, HSBC and others are saying
articles.content: Coal India's shares surged following a stable March quarter performance, with a 12% rise in consolidated profit after tax to Rs 10,908 crore. Revenue also climbed 6% to Rs 46,490 crore, driven by better realisations. Analysts remain divided, with Jefferies and Motilal Oswal maintaining Buy ratings, while Morgan Stanley and HSBC suggest holding the stock.
articles.excerpt: Coal India's shares surged following a stable March quarter performance, with a 12% rise in consolidated profit after tax to Rs 10,908 crore. Revenue also climbed 6% to Rs 46,490 crore, driven by better realisations. Analysts remain divided, with Jefferies and Motilal Oswal maintaining Buy ratings, while Morgan Stanley and HSBC suggest holding the stock.

articles.title: Reliance share price target hiked to Rs 1,910: Why Goldman, CLSA, and Morgan Stanley are betting big
articles.content: Reliance Industries is gaining traction as Goldman Sachs raises its target price, with global brokerages predicting significant upside despite a recent 13% stock dip. Analysts are optimistic about the oil-to-chemicals business recovery, the impending Jio IPO, and Reliance's integrated downstream advantage in a tightening market.
